items.title: Algal bloom information 'inconsistent', amid survey of health impacts of the bloom

children.content: Volunteers surveyed more than 200 people who reported a range of symptoms during the algal bloom outbreak not represented in the SA government's health advice. A disability advocate says health information has been "inaccessible" and "inconsistent".

gallery.altText: A mother and son stand in front of an ocean looking at the camera

thumbnail.altText: A mother and son stand in front of an ocean looking at the camera

items.title: Feud erupts as consumers on the hook for billion-dollar grid blowout

children.content: Transgrid wants to claw back huge cost increases for a major transmission line from consumers. Experts say the regulator's decision will have far-reaching consequences.

image.altText: Men working to construct a large transmission line tower in southern NSW

items.title: Accused murderer 'poisoned parents to death' with insulin, court hears

children.content: Adelaide woman Raelene Polymiadis is standing trial for two counts of murder after allegedly deliberately administering "fatal doses of insulin" to her parents in separate incidents, just over a year apart.

image.altText: A woman wearing sunglasses walks along a footpath with a man.

items.title: Dog rescued after being trapped for five days in breakwater

children.content: A 14-year-old fox terrier is back in her owner's arms after a multi-day stint trapped in the Glenelg breakwater after she broke free from her leash.

image.altText: A woman with short grey hair wearing a blue shirt and vest smiling and cuddling a small black, white and brown dog.

items.title: Lancaster bomber pilot and Riverland volunteer Howard Hendrick remembered

children.content: Howard Hendrick's life was dedicated to service in the Royal Australian Air Force and to his community. His contributions will forever remembered by his family and his hometown.

image.altText: Framed photos are stacked on top of each other showing a man flying a plane and getting married.

items.title: 'Smiling since last night': Community joy after Irankunda's World Cup goal

children.content: The family and community of Socceroos star Nestory Irankunda are "very emotional" after his goal-scoring World Cup debut. His cousin says he will be "forever grateful" for the support the family received after arriving in Australia, and "their blessing is seeing this at the big stage".

articles.title: Socceroos boast heavy Adelaide connection, as SA fans show support

children.content: Thousands of South Australian soccer fans descended on The Drive in Adelaide city to watch Adelaide's own Nestory Irankunda score the first half-opener, leading to Australia's win over Türkiye on Sunday.

image.altText: thousand of fans look up at a TV screen

articles.title: When Irankunda ran to the corner to emulate Cahill, it was a pre-planned tribute

children.content: Nestory Irankunda's childhood hero is none other than Tim Cahill. So it was fitting that more than 20 years after Cahill's famous celebration, this generation's Socceroos hero paid tribute.

image.altText: Nestory Irankunda punches the corner flag

articles.title: 'A footballer's dream': From humble beginnings to the world stage

children.content: The Touré brothers snuck into their local football field after hours to play the game they loved, while the Yengi brothers kicked around makeshift balls made of plastic bags. Two of them have now made it onto the world stage as Socceroos.

image.altText: composite of a Mo Toure as a young boy in a soccer field and him as an adult in a Socceroos guernsey

gallery.altText: Two soccer players in green and gold celebrate a goal

thumbnail.altText: Two soccer players in green and gold celebrate a goal

items.title: Charges dropped against 'masked man' after fire at popular tourism site

children.content: A man who was charged after a fire near Mount Gambier will still have to appear in court to face harm, theft and offensive language charges.

image.altText: Flames on the side of a road behind a guardrail on a sunny day

items.title: SA Police charge man over alleged possession of mobile phone gun

children.content: South Australian police have separately arrested two men for the alleged possession of a handgun disguised as a mobile phone, and a homemade "pen gun".

image.altText: A red mobile phone-looking item split in half to reveal a black trigger in the middle of the top half.

items.title: Concerns new BHP agreement 'locks in' basin water extraction

children.content: Environmentalists and First Nations groups say a new mining agreement with BHP does not do enough to end water extraction from the Great Artesian Basin. But the SA government says the agreement imposes stricter environmental regulation.

image.altText: An overhead view of a BHP wellfield in outback South Australia.

items.title: 'Lives in danger' as SA Bureau of Meteorology wave buoy fails yet again

children.content: The wave buoy off Kangaroo Island is the only such device operated by the Bureau of Meteorology in South Australia and is relied upon by professional divers and fishers.

image.altText: An orange wave buoy rider in the ocean

items.title: Why young people say the social media ban is not working six months on

children.content: Research suggests many under-16s are still active on social media platforms. BTN High explores why and tests the accuracy of popular technology used to estimate age.

gallery.altText: A teenage boy holds up a mobile phone with the number 16 on it. Logos for Facebook, X and other platforms swirl near his face.

thumbnail.altText: A teenage boy holds up a mobile phone with the number 16 on it. Logos for Facebook, X and other platforms swirl near his face.

items.title: The multi-million-dollar tourist hotspot where locals can't afford to live

children.content: A tourist hotspot with skyrocketing rents, low rental vacancies and a dearth of public housing, has found a way to help find homes for those who can't afford to live in their own region.

image.altText: A young woman with flowing hair stands amid the leaves and flowering gumnuts of a eucalypt tree

items.title: T-Birds cap off near-flawless season with record number of wins

children.content: This year's record-breaking Adelaide Thunderbirds are shifting their focus to the Super Netball finals, with a third premiership in four years on offer.

image.altText: A Thunderbirds player and Lightning player reach across each other for the ball

items.title: From wine to water, Adelaide Hills charity brings hope to kids in Kenya

children.content: From the Adelaide Hills to refugee camps in Kenya, how one young Aussie has made it his mission to help.

image.altText: A group of young boys smile and pose for a photo in rural Kenya.

items.title: SANFL players consider industrial action as hourly salary drops to $8

children.content: The SANFL Players' Association is discussing the potential of a strike in response to a 50 per cent decline in pay over the past 20 years.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie sitting on a bench in a grassed area

items.title: 'Never crossed my mind he wouldn't survive': Seven die daily from drug overdose

children.content: Australia recorded its deadliest year on record for drug overdoses, according to a new analysis, with an average of seven deaths per day.

gallery.altText: A woman sits on a park bench holding a photo of her and her son in a picture frame.

thumbnail.altText: A woman sits on a park bench holding a photo of her and her son in a picture frame.

items.title: Brother believes pregnant 20yo could have been Ivan Milat's first victim

children.content: A family says its private investigation suggests Keren Rowland's 1971 disappearance and death is linked to serial killer Ivan Milat as an inquiry hears claims of missing records, lost exhibits and unanswered questions.

image.altText: A shotgun-toting man with a handlebar moustache and broad-brimmed hat poses for a photo.

items.title: Senator Duniam's resignation could open door for One Nation

children.content: Tasmanian senator Jonno Duniam's announcement that he intends to leave politics could be the leg up that One Nation needs.

image.altText: A woman speaks in front of a crowd.

items.title: Police watchdog review imminent amid 'unfair public attack' accusation

children.content: The police watchdog is set to deliver its findings into an investigation of the response to Lindy Lucena's death after her family called for scrutiny.

image.altText: Lindy Lucena

items.title: AFP begins inquiry into allegations Israeli forces assaulted flotilla activists

children.content: Australian Federal Police have begun inquiries into allegations that Israeli defence personnel sexually assaulted Australian activists on a flotilla headed to Gaza, following a meeting with Foreign Minister Penny Wong.

image.altText: Four women sit at a wooden bench, looking serious.

items.title: 'Eye for an eye': How Captain Cook met his bloody end in Hawaii

children.content: Captain Cook set sail from Plymouth to find an elusive trade route linking Europe to Asia in 1776. He never saw England again.

gallery.altText: A coloured artwork depicting Captain Cook being stabbed by Hawaiians in the 1700s.
Captain Cook sits for a portrait while sat at a table on which sits his hat. He looks serious

thumbnail.altText: A coloured artwork depicting Captain Cook being stabbed by Hawaiians in the 1700s.
Captain Cook sits for a portrait while sat at a table on which sits his hat. He looks serious

items.title: Legendary band Australian Crawl reforms for first time in 40 years

children.content: Australian Crawl will lead a major line-up of classic Australian acts across a run of shows later this year.

image.altText: Australian Crawl members hold Sons of Beaches award plaques against a wood-paneled wall

items.title: The AFL teams that are holding their nerve the best in clutch moments

children.content: It's been a season of tight finishes in the AFL, but which teams are holding their nerve the best and why?

image.altText: Adam Treloar

items.title: Five reasons to try to save cash, even if you're struggling

children.content: Saving is not the only tool you need to achieve financial security these days, but it still has a role to play if you can manage it.

gallery.altText: Two hands hold a piggy bank full of coins.

thumbnail.altText: Two hands hold a piggy bank full of coins.

avatar.alt: Lacey Filipich stands against a wall, smiling, wearing a white top and denim jacket.

items.title: With the success of 007 First Light, the future of James Bond is looking bright

children.content: While producers are still mulling over who will take the Bond mantle from Daniel Craig, the super-spy has already moved on in 007 First Light.

image.altText: A screenshot from 007 First Light

items.title: Quiz: Can you pick Ireland's flag in a line-up? It's harder than you think

children.content: Which country's flag is the only one with a modern weapon on it? And which US state has the Union Jack on its flag?

image.altText: Two lines of flags from dozens of countries.

items.title: Two men convicted of plot to torch property linked to British PM Keir Starmer

children.content: A Russian-speaking figure who went by the name "El Money" offered one of the men money to torch the properties and record video that could be posted online to draw attention to the attack.

image.altText: The mugshots of two young men side by side.

items.title: Are Australia's environment laws still 'broken'?

children.content: The clearing of koala habitat at a Brisbane school has enraged advocates and raised questions about the federal government's sweeping changes to national environment laws.

image.altText: A koala climbing a tree

items.title: Hospital call from Melbourne's outer north leaders

children.content: A group of councils in Melbourne's north is calling on the state government to spend $45 million to buy land to begin planning for a hospital and train station.

gallery.altText: Man in blue blazer and white shirt standing next to man in navy jumper

thumbnail.altText: Man in blue blazer and white shirt standing next to man in navy jumper

items.title: Cairns man charged after allegedly naming man at centre of extortion case

children.content: A Cairns man accused of exposing the identity of a Queensland public figure at the centre of a mysterious extortion case has been charged by police with allegedly disobeying a suppression order.

image.altText: A designed image with the Cairns Courthouse and a silhouette of a man on red background.

items.title: Labor's CGT plan flawed but better than what we have, economists tell inquiry

children.content: Economists have offered qualified support for the government's plans on day one of a Senate inquiry.

image.altText: Albanese is in focus as he sits on the green bench watching Chalmers speak

items.title: PM to make fuel excise call early next week

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says the government will give Australians "appropriate notice" about the fuel excise rebate, as it continues to monitor the issue.

image.altText: A close up of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a white hard hat and a yellow high-vis vest.

items.title: 'Sharp increase' in drone strikes leads to 1,000 civilian deaths in Sudan

children.content: The United Nations has condemned an increase in drone attacks in conflict-torn Sudan.

image.altText: Four men, wearing white robes, stand, looking at a mass grave in the dirt, covered with branches.

items.title: UK plans to ban social media for under-16s

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer says he will ban social media sites for under-16s and impose restrictions on gaming and live streaming platforms in some of the most far-reaching online restrictions globally.

gallery.altText: Keir Starmer wearing a black suit and teal tie, mid-speech while sitting at a wooden table and gesturing with his right hand.
